nameMeanings.name: NOT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Czeslawa, Zbigniew, Zygmunt, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Danuta, Dariusz, Dymitr, Elzbieta, Halina, Janina, Janusz.  Jewish Shlomo, Avraham.

Polish, Czech, Belorussian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Polish, Czech, and Belorussian kot вҖҳtom catвҖҷ, a nickname denoting either a cautious or a skillful person, or a seducer. As a Jewish name it is generally artificial. The homonymous Sorbian cognate KГіt (from Lower Sorbian kГіt вҖҳtom catвҖҷ) is found in Germanized forms, only (see Koth , Kothe , Kott , and Kotte ). Compare also Cott 4.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname и‘ӣ, see   Ge   1.

variants.name: Kotas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jozef, Justyna, Piotr, Wojciech.
Czech: from a pet form of the personal name ChotДӣbor, meaning вҖҳwarlikeвҖҷ.

variants.name: Kotowicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Czeslaw, Kazimierz, Krzysztof, Szczepan, Wladyslaw, Zdzislaw.
Polish and Jewish (from Poland): patronymic from   Kot  .

variants.name: Koth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt, Gerhard, Lorenz, Oskar.
German (also KГ¶th): from Middle High German, Middle Low German kote вҖҳcottage, hovelвҖҷ, a status name for a day laborer who lived in a cottage and owned no farmland. Compare Kotz .
German: from a short form of any of various personal names formed with Old High German got вҖҳgodвҖҷ, for example Gottfried.

nameMeanings.name: IN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Kyung, Sung, Chan, Duk, Eun Suk, Hee, Hyun, Hyung, Ik, Jae, Kon, Kwang Won, Chang, Chung, Chong, Hyun Jin, Inki, Jeong, Jin Tae, Joo, Myong, Ok Soon, Poon, Poong.  Southeast Asian Hung, Khen, My, Phan, Tek, Thang, Tok.

Korean: name of a family that migrated from China to Korea during the reign of Shilla's 3rd-century king Sok Yu-rye (284вҖ“298). The founding ancestor, In So, established a clan seat in the Kyodong area of Cholla North Province. Another clan seat was subsequently established by his descendent, In Pin, during the Koryo period. The surname is not common in Korea.

variants.name: Yin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Feng, Hong, Jian, Kong, Li, Man, Min, Ning, Yuan, Bei, Chao, Ching, Chong, Chung, Heng, Yeng, Tun, Youn.  Cambodian Hai, Phal, Phon, Pu, Samnang, Tho, Thy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname ж®·, meaning вҖҳplentifulвҖҷ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (ж®·), the name of the capital (located in present-day Henan province) of the Shang dynasty (1600вҖ“1046 BC ). Pan Geng, the 19th king of the Shang dynasty, moved the original capital Yan (located in present-day Shandong province) to Yin. Thus the Shang dynasty was also known as the Yin dynasty. After the Shang dynasty was overthrown by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087вҖ“1043 BC ), people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from Yin Shui (ж®·ж°ҙ, вҖҳRiver YinвҖҷ), the name of a river in Shangshui in Henan Province. People who lived along the river acquired ж®· (Yin), the name of the river, as their surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname е°№, a term for the official title in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (е°№), the name of a fief (said to have been located in Shexian in Shanxi province or XinвҖҷan in Henan province), which was granted to Yin, the son of the legendary king Shao Hao (c. 26th century BC ). (ii) from the personal name of Shi Yin (её«е°№), literally meaning вҖҳhead of officersвҖҷ, the name of an official post during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046вҖ“771 BC ). (iii) from the second element of Ling Yin (д»Өе°№), the name of an official post in charge of the military in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770вҖ“476 BC ). This post was held by a prince of the state of Shen called Cheng, who migrated to the state of Chu when he took up the post.
